paper_title: Soil Analysis of the Experimental Site

methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing soil samples from the experimental site to determine physiochemical parameters.

steps.description: Soil samples of 0 to 30 cm depth were taken from five spots from the area of the experimental site (100 g/spot) during the season of study before starting the experiment.
steps.searchable_summary: collect soil samples from five spots at 0-30 cm depth

steps.description: The samples were carefully combined into one sample to ascertain the physiochemical parameters of the soil that was used.
steps.searchable_summary: combine soil samples into one composite sample

steps.description: A mortar and pestle were used to ground the soil air-dried samples, which were then passed in a stainless steel test sieve into fractions <2 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: grind and sieve soil samples to <2 mm

methods.description: Procedures for analyzing soil texture and chemical properties using various methods and instruments.

steps.description: The particle size distribution was analyzed using the hydrometer method.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze particle size distribution using hydrometer method

steps.description: The soil pH, EC, organic matter (O.M) %, sodium adsorption ratio (SAR), and exchangeable sodium percentage (ESP) % were measured.
steps.searchable_summary: measure soil pH, EC, O.M %, SAR, and ESP %

steps.description: The soil soluble cations Na+, K+, Mg++, Ca++, and Zn++ were measured.
steps.searchable_summary: measure soil soluble cations Na+, K+, Mg++, Ca++, Zn++

steps.description: The land soil-soluble anions CO3--, Cl-, HCO3-, and SO4 2- were measured.
steps.searchable_summary: measure soil-soluble anions CO3--, Cl-, HCO3-, SO4 2-

steps.description: For this determination, the following soil chemical parameters were employed: 20 g dried soil/100 mL distilled water (1:5), and were left for 24 h; then, the extract was filtered.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare soil extract with 20 g soil in 100 mL water, filter after 24 h

steps.description: An EC meter was used to determine soil EC.
steps.searchable_summary: determine soil EC using EC meter

steps.description: Jackson's method was applied for the estimation of Ca++, Mg++, and Cl-.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate Ca++, Mg++, Cl- using Jackson's method

steps.description: Total carbonate and organic matter were quantified using a specific method.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify total carbonate and organic matter

steps.description: The micro Kjeldahl method was used for estimating the level of available N.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate available N using micro Kjeldahl method

steps.description: Available P was estimated using a specific method.
Na+ and K+ were determined with the PSC7 flame photometer.
steps.searchable_summary: determine Na+ and K+ using PSC7 flame photometer

steps.description: SAR and ESP were also estimated.
Zn++ was quantified with the atomic absorption spectrophotometer (AAS).
steps.searchable_summary: quantify Zn++ using atomic absorption spectrophotometer

steps.description: Soil pH was measured in soil suspension (1:2.5, soil: distilled water) after 30 min using a pH meter.
steps.searchable_summary: measure soil pH in suspension using pH meter
